page_content:
Formerly: ENCH610.

CHBE620 Methods of Engineering Analysis (3 Credits)

Application of selected mathematical techniques to the analysis and solution of engineering problems; included are the applications of matrices, vectors, tensors, differential equations, integral transforms, and probability methods to such problems as unsteady heat transfer, transient phenomena in mass transfer operations, stagewise processes, chemical reactors, process control, and nuclear reactor physics. Prerequisite: MATH246 and CHBE250; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department. Credit Only Granted for. ENCH620 or CHBE620.
